sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check where the player moved now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after second RIGHT
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in after:
    if n['id'] == 6:
        print(f"Player boundary: {n['boundary']}")
    if n['id'] == 8:
        print(f"Player center: {n['boundary']}")

# Check the area around the player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nPlayer area (rows 8-12, cols 25-45):")
for i in range(8, 13):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][25:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player moved from cols 20-24 to cols 26-30 (another 6 columns right). The player is now at cols 26-30, rows 8-12. Looking at the area, I can see the red path (RRR) at cols 39-41. The player is approaching the red area.
Let me think about the maze structure:
- The player moves in 6-column increments horizontally
- The player is a 4x4 box
- The white corridors are 5 cells wide
Looking at the maze more carefully:
- Row 14: BBBBBBBWWWWWBBBBBBBWWWWWWWBRB
  - White corridors at cols 20-24 and cols 32-37
  - The red vertical corridor is at col 39-40
The player is currently at cols 26-30. If I try RIGHT again, the player would move to cols 32-36, which aligns with the white corridor at cols 32-37.
But wait - the player is at rows 8-12, which is ABOVE the white corridors at row 14+. The player seems to be moving along the top edge of the black region.
Let me try to understand the maze better. Looking at the ASCII:
- Row 7: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB (top wall)
- Row 8-12: Player area with blue box
- Row 13: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBRBBB
- Row 14: BBBBBBBWWWWWBBBBBBBWWWWWWWBRB
So the player is in a corridor at the top of the maze. The white areas below are additional corridors.
Let me try RIGHT again to move further right, then try DOWN when aligned with a white corridor.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at cols 26-30. Let me try RIGHT again to get to cols 32-36
# Then try DOWN to enter the white corridor
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
